title: TypeScript build fails: Cannot find module '../lib/tsc.js'
evidence: npm run build fails with: Error: Cannot find module '../lib/tsc.js' at node_modules/.bin/tsc:2:1
suggestion: Check typescript package installation; may require npm install or clearing node_modules and reinstalling

title: POST /books — Create a new book (title, author, year, isbn)
evidence: src/app.ts:65-76 implements POST /books with full validation; tests/books.test.ts:23-67 covers creation and validation

title: GET /books — List all books (support ?author= filter)
evidence: src/app.ts:78-89 implements GET /books with author filtering; tests/books.test.ts:70-95 tests listing and filtering

title: GET /books/{id} — Get a single book by ID
evidence: src/app.ts:91-101 implements GET /books/:id with 404 handling; tests/books.test.ts:98-114 tests retrieval and error cases

title: PUT /books/{id} — Update a book
evidence: src/app.ts:103-126 implements PUT /books/:id with validation and 404 handling; tests/books.test.ts:117-137 tests updates

title: DELETE /books/{id} — Delete a book
evidence: src/app.ts:128-138 implements DELETE /books/:id with 404 handling; tests/books.test.ts:140-153 tests deletion

title: Use specified language and framework (TypeScript)
evidence: package.json and tsconfig.json configure TypeScript; all source files in src/ are .ts files

title: Store data in SQLite (via better-sqlite3)
evidence: src/db.ts:1-24 creates Database and initializes books table using better-sqlite3

title: Return JSON responses with appropriate HTTP status codes
Input validation (title and author are required)
evidence: src/app.ts:19-49 validateBook() function enforces non-empty title and author; rejects with 400

title: Health check endpoint: GET /health
evidence: src/app.ts:61-63 implements GET /health returning {status: 'ok'}; tests/books.test.ts:14-20 verifies it

title: Working source code in workspace directory
evidence: src/ contains app.ts, server.ts, db.ts; all code is syntactically valid TypeScript (but cannot be verified as executable due to build failure)

title: README.md with setup and run instructions
evidence: README.md:1-104 provides comprehensive setup, run, test, endpoints, and example instructions

title: At least 3 unit/integration tests
evidence: tests/books.test.ts contains 18 test cases across 6 describe blocks; covers all endpoints and error cases

title: Tests cannot be verified due to build failure
evidence: npm test would fail because npm run build fails first
suggestion: Fix the TypeScript build issue before attempting to verify test results
